title: Arminius
text: Home * Engines * Arminius Arminius Source [1] Arminius , a chess engine by Volker Annuss , which is a complete restructured successor of Hermann [2] . Both engines, Hermann and Arminius use a neural network to calculate a probability how likely it is in iterative deepening that the best move will change when searching one ply deeper . They both use properties of the position and results from previous iterations that are correlated with a change in the best move in the next iteration. Arminius also considers relative node counts [3] . Arminius' tournament debut was the DOCCC 2011 . Arminius 2017, the version that won the third place at PT 51 , is using its own format of 6-men WDL endgame bitbases [4] . In August 2017, Arminius started to practice Black Magic [5] .
